steps.title: Calculate the number of doublings and set up the equation
steps.content: From 1660 to 1760 is 100 years. Since the population doubles every 25 years, the number of doubling periods is: $$\frac{100}{25} = 4$$ After 4 doublings, the population is multiplied by $2^4 = 16$. If $P$ is the 1660 population: $$P \times 16 = 208000$$

steps.title: Solve for the initial population
steps.content: Divide both sides by 16: $$P = \frac{208000}{16} = 13000$$ The population in 1660 was 13,000. That matches choice A — A is correct.
